subject: [PATCH 05/11] mm/zsmalloc: Store obj_cgroup pointer in zspage

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Identified a potential bug where writing to uninitialized memory could occur if pool->memcg_aware is not set. Suggested adding a conditional statement to avoid the write and prevent crashes.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Acknowledged the potential issue and agreed to make changes in the next version. Also mentioned sprinkling WARN_ON_ONCEs in other places.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, 11 Mar 2026 13:17:26 -0700 Yosry Ahmed <yosry@kernel.org> wrote:

> [..]
> > @@ -1216,6 +1216,11 @@ void zs_obj_write(struct zs_pool *pool, unsigned long handle,
> >         class = zspage_class(pool, zspage);
> >         off = offset_in_page(class->size * obj_idx);
> >
> > +       if (objcg) {
> > +               WARN_ON_ONCE(!pool->memcg_aware);
> > +               zspage->objcgs[obj_idx] = objcg;
> > +       }

Hello Yosry,

I hope you are doing well. Thank you for reviewing this series! : -)

> If pool->memcg_aware is not set the warning will fire, but the
> following line will write to uninitialized memory and probably crash.
> We should avoid the write if the warning fires.
> 
> Maybe:
> 
> if (objcg && !WARN_ON_ONCE(!pool->memcg_aware))
>        zspage->objcgs[obj_idx] = objcg;

Ack. 

> Not pretty, but the same pattern is followed in many places in the kernel.
> 
> > +
> >         if (!ZsHugePage(zspage))
> >                 off += ZS_HANDLE_SIZE;
> >

Definitely better than writing garbage and crashing : -)
I'll make this change in the next version, I think I should also sprinkle
these WARN_ON_ONCEs in a few other places as well. I'll be more
mindful of this for those cases as well.

Thank you again Yosry, I hope you have a great day!
Joshua

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch series aims to store the obj_cgroup pointer in zspage, enabling the propagation of object cgroups from the zswap/zram layer down to zsmalloc. The patch modifies several kernel files, including drivers/block/zram/zram_drv.c and mm/zsmalloc.c, to handle the lifetime and charging of the obj_cgroup.
